title: viola × sabuletorum
intro: Viola × sabuletorum, also known as the hybrid marsh violet, is a flowering plant that is a cross between Viola palustris and Viola riviniana. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola × sabuletorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe sore throats, and ease cold and flu symptoms. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for overall well-being.
